title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marco Sturm criticized the Bruins for underestimating the shorthanded Florida Panthers in their 2-1 loss.
Despite being reigning champions, the injury-hit Panthers struggled this season, entering the game with a 36-35-3 record.
The Bruins' playoff chances remain high at 95%, despite losing momentum before the playoffs begin in a few weeks.

title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied from Reading, Mass. sentenced to 8 months for illegal trafficking of more than 100 endangered animal parts.
Bied pleaded guilty to smuggling wildlife parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, violating the Lacey Act and other laws.
He was fined $75,000 and ordered to fund wildlife enforcement efforts, following illegal sales to undercover agents.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black was convicted of first-degree murder for the fatal stabbing of Milteer Hendricks in Falmouth in 2023.
Black, aged 22 at the time, attacked Hendricks with a group, stabbing him in the chest outside an apartment complex.
The conviction carries a mandatory life sentence without parole; sentencing is scheduled for Friday.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia was suspended with pay after being named in a Middlesex DA report on Sgt. Scott Quigley's conduct.
Sgt. Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent homicide while driving under the influence, resulting in a fatal crash.
DeLucia allegedly instructed DA's security director to keep Quigley's intoxication at the crash secret, without reporting it.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter in foster child's 2020 death.
Infant died from untreated medical conditions despite clear symptoms, leading to manslaughter and reckless child endangerment convictions.
Sentences staggered due to couple's four other children; barred from future caregiving roles except for their own children.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, sentenced to life without parole for poisoning boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in Salisbury, Massachusetts.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder after Fowler died of ethylene glycol poisoning in 2022, following a tumultuous relationship.
Prosecutors cited jealousy over Fowler's affair as motive; Church plans to appeal her conviction, according to court documents.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have started their 2026 season with a disappointing 1-5 record, including a sweep by the Astros.
Boston's pitching staff ranks low in performance metrics, with a team ERA of 4.91 and high home runs allowed.
Top new acquisitions like Willson Contreras and Caleb Durbin have struggled offensively, with low batting averages and high strikeouts.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, announced his retirement from the NFL after 13 seasons with six different teams.
Gilmore's career highlights include a Super Bowl win and the NFL Defensive Player of the Year award in 2019.
He played for the Patriots from 2017 to 2021 and recorded 617 tackles and 32 interceptions in his career.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle has started global layoffs, impacting employees across multiple countries as part of organizational changes.
Analysts estimate up to 30,000 Oracle employees may be laid off, but no official confirmation from the company.
The impact on Oracle's Massachusetts workforce remains unclear, with no layoffs reported in recent state filings.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, his third team in three years.
Dugger recorded 42 tackles, two interceptions, and one sack in nine games with the Steelers last season.
The former Patriots captain spent five-and-a-half seasons with New England before moving to Pittsburgh and now Cincinnati.
